caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role weights are supplied at 4-digit 5414, not 541430.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 27-1025 Interior Designers row is known sibling-code contamination and is not treated as representative of the target code.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: No task-frequency survey maps automation shares directly to these wage shares.
inputs.pi_dist: No code-level buyer survey measures differentiation.
inputs.pi_moat: This is a service moat judgment, not a claim of transferable proprietary technology.
inputs.t50_years: Adobe's sample is creative professionals and other creatives across three countries, not a U.S. NAICS establishment census.
inputs.t50_years: Daily use is not a workflow-weighted effective-adoption measure; the separately reported pragmatic segment is not combined with it.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The source does not publish a 541430-specific all-frequency adoption rate.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Daily use among surveyed graphic designers is a conservative proxy, not a representative workflow-weighted measure of effective adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: No code-specific historical price series was located.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No 541430 owner-age distribution was found.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Do not convert generic closure and uncertainty data into a code-specific acquisition pipeline.
inputs.active_consolidators: The report's transaction set extends beyond pure graphic design and includes larger agencies.
inputs.buy_mult: Not a transaction-comp-derived 541430 multiple; no investment recommendation follows.
inputs.exit_mult: This is an underwriting stress assumption, not an observed valuation statistic.
inputs.pricing_structure: Marketplace historical contracts are not a census of U.S. agency billing.

Capture and exit assumptions partly reuse client substitution, project-pricing pressure and weak-portability mechanisms; this may over-penalize C and M even though the low-retention and melting-demand directions remain useful.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $191,989 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541430); class <5: 13,359 firms, avg revenue/firm $259,220 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $40,568 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,416 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,112,405 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $174,091 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 656 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,308,331 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $361,254 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 318 firms, avg revenue/firm $7,449,481 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,165,844 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 30 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,280,067 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,860,830 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 22 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,342,682 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,870,630 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 370

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Weighted exposure estimate based on software-centered graphic-design tasks and current creative-tool use. It preserves every immutable role share, discounts the contaminated Interior Designers row, and assigns a high but uncertain fraction to the unlisted residual because it likely contains junior production and adjacent design labor.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541430 ($5,155,227,877) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541430 ($9,873,226,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

Capture and exit assumptions partly reuse client substitution, project-pricing pressure and weak-portability mechanisms; this may over-penalize C and M even though the low-retention and melting-demand directions remain useful.
source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact mechanics, identity, model routing, manifest digests, deterministic dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. Every registered source is accessible: Adobe supports the 40.9% daily-use proxy but not representative effective adoption or t50; BLS and Upwork support the core task, pricing and demand-risk directions; the M&A and succession sources are broad proxies whose limits and estimate status are disclosed. The packet avoids the prior adoption-segment aggregation error, keeps t50 positive below the chosen proxy boundary, preserves the contaminated 4-digit role mix rather than mutating it, and presents a useful low-confidence kill screen. The capture/exit/terminal mechanisms overlap enough to warrant an additional visible double-counting caveat, but the reopened evidence does not show that this overlap alone is likely to reverse the thesis without also changing multiple independent low-retention and exit judgments.

sources.title: Adobe, How Creatives are Thinking About AI
sources.what_it_supports: June 2026 survey of nearly 2,000 creatives: 38% daily AI use overall; graphic designers lead the listed roles with 40.9% daily use; the largest segment is mostly weekly users. Used for adoption timing and workflow exposure, not NAICS revenue share.

sources.title: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, Graphic Designers Occupational Outlook Handbook
sources.what_it_supports: Defines graphic-design tasks such as layouts, logos, concepts, client revisions and final production; reports 2% projected employment growth for 2024–34 and notes AI may reduce companies' need to contract with freelance graphic designers.

sources.title: Upwork, Graphic Designer Hourly Rates
sources.what_it_supports: Marketplace indication of $15–$35 median hourly rates, a wider $15–$150 range, and common fixed-price engagements. Used only to characterize transparent, project-based pricing and global freelance alternatives.

sources.title: J&A Sell-Side M&A Report, Independent Creative Agencies M&A Transactions and Valuations
sources.what_it_supports: Broad independent-creative-agency M&A context through Q4 2024, including the distinction between technology-driven agencies and traditional service models and examples of strategic acquisitions. It is not a pure-play 541430 small-firm valuation comp.

sources.title: Gallup, Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.what_it_supports: Fall-2024 survey of U.S. business owners showing nonemployer businesses are less likely to expect a sale or transfer and more likely to expect closure or uncertainty. Used only as generic small-owner succession context.

title: Graphic Design Services
